description: MTurk HIT configuration for hate speech annotation. Contains regulatory context on IRB requirements and worker welfare obligations.
pi: Dr. S. Chowdhury, NLP Lab, Department of Computer Science

hit_configuration.task_description: You will be asked to label social media posts for the presence of hate speech, target groups, severity, and confidence. Some posts may contain offensive, hateful, or violent language.

content_types: Posts containing offensive language or slurs (approx. 35%)
Posts containing explicit hate speech targeting protected groups (approx. 15%)
Posts containing threats or incitement to violence (approx. 10%)
annotation_dimensions: Target group (multi-label: race/ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, disability, other, none)

common_rule_45_cfr_46.applicability: 45 CFR 46 (the Common Rule) applies to research involving living humans through systematic interaction or intervention, including interaction via digital platforms. Contractor or payment status does not determine human subjects status.
common_rule_45_cfr_46.irb_requirement: Research involving human subjects requires IRB review and approval (or documented exemption determination) before data collection begins.
common_rule_45_cfr_46.relevant_excerpt: Research means a systematic investigation, including research development, testing, and evaluation, designed to develop or contribute to generalizable knowledge. Activities that meet this definition constitute research for purposes of this policy, whether or not they are conducted or supported under a program that is considered research for other purposes. For example, some demonstration and service programs may include research activities.

worker_welfare.occupational_health_evidence: Peer-reviewed research documents that content moderators and annotators exposed to harmful content experience psychological distress, including intrusive thoughts, burnout, and PTSD-like symptoms. (Steiger et al., 2021, CHI, DOI: 10.1145/3411764.3445092; Roberts, 2019, Behind the Screen, Yale University Press; Miceli et al., 2022, Proc. ACM Hum.-Comput. Interact., DOI: 10.1145/3492853.)
worker_welfare.minimum_wage_note: At $0.05 per HIT and an estimated 30 seconds per HIT, the implied hourly rate is $6.00/hour. Federal minimum wage is $7.25/hour. State minimum wages as of 2026-04-13 are: California $16.90/hour, New York City $17.00/hour (with $16.00/hour in the remainder of the state), and Washington $17.13/hour. This compensation falls significantly below both federal and state minimum wage levels.
